name: Taft Jordan
abstract: Taft Jordan (February 15, 1915 – December 1, 1981) was an American jazz trumpeter.

about_quotes.speaker: Al Hall
about_quotes.evidence: Lips Page I worked with, Taft Jordan -- Taft was the swinging musician that -- of the period that I just started playing bass in Philadelphia when he was working with Doc Holder's band.

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Edison
about_quotes.evidence: Had another trumpet player named Rex Stewart, he was fantastic, Taft Jordan, Bobby played with Chick Webb, Bobby Starks, great trumpet player.
about_quotes.doc_title: Harry Edison — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Buddy Tate
about_quotes.evidence: So I left and finally, she got a good saxophone player that, you know, that was a regular, Taft Jordan and Tyree was in the band, Tyree Glenn.

about_quotes.speaker: Buck Clayton
about_quotes.evidence: And two good examples of that was Taft Jordan and Shad Collins. To me they played like all the trumpet players played in New York, the style.

about_quotes.speaker: Teddy McRae
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ... Renauld Jones, Taft Jordan, Sandy, Pete Clark, Edgar Sampson, Elmer Williams, Truehart, John Kirby. ... [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah.

about_quotes.speaker: Al Grey
about_quotes.evidence: so he signed me on to play in the studio band the first black trombone player. They had at that time they had Taft Jordan and Billy Kyle
about_quotes.doc_title: Al Grey interviewed by Michael Woods, Scottsdale, Arizona, March 3, 1995
